questions.text: What are the primary goals of an operating system according to the provided material?

choices.label: To execute user programs and make solving user problems easier.
choices.text: To execute user programs and make solving user problems easier.

choices.label: To make the computer system convenient to use.
choices.text: To make the computer system convenient to use.

choices.label: To use the computer hardware in an efficient manner.
choices.text: To use the computer hardware in an efficient manner.

questions.explanation: The slide explicitly lists executing user programs, providing convenience, and ensuring hardware efficiency as the three main goals of an OS.

questions.text: Identify the four main components of a computer system. (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The system is divided into Hardware, OS, Application Programs, and Users. Firmware is a specific type of storage for the bootstrap program but not one of the four high-level components.

questions.text: From a system view, the operating system is primarily defined as a:
questions.explanation: The system view defines the OS as a resource allocator (managing hardware/software conflicts) and a control program (managing program execution to prevent errors).

questions.text: Which of the following is the 'one program running at all times on the computer'?
questions.explanation: The kernel is defined as the central component that runs continuously. Other programs are either system programs (bundled with the OS) or applications.

questions.text: Where is the bootstrap program typically stored during power-up or reboot?

choices.label: In Read-Only Memory (ROM) or EPROM, known as firmware
choices.text: In Read-Only Memory (ROM) or EPROM, known as firmware

questions.explanation: Initial boot code (bootstrap) is stored in non-volatile ROM or EPROM (firmware) to initialize the system and load the kernel.

questions.text: In a modern computer system, how does a device controller inform the CPU that it has finished its operation?

choices.label: By modifying a variable in shared memory
choices.text: By modifying a variable in shared memory

questions.explanation: Device controllers use interrupts to signal the CPU that an I/O operation is complete, allowing concurrent execution.

questions.text: A software-generated interrupt caused either by an error or a specific user request is called a:
questions.explanation: A trap (or exception) is the specific term used for interrupts triggered by software conditions like division by zero or system calls.

questions.text: When an interrupt occurs, what must the interrupt architecture do first before servicing the interrupt?

choices.label: Save the address of the interrupted instruction
choices.text: Save the address of the interrupted instruction

questions.explanation: The system must save the state (registers and program counter) so it can return to the exact same point in the user program after handling the interrupt.

questions.text: Calculate the number of bytes in 1 Gigabyte (GB) according to standard computer storage definitions.
questions.explanation: In binary storage notation, KB is \( 1,024^1 \), MB is \( 1,024^2 \), and GB is \( 1,024^3 \).

questions.text: Which storage medium is directly accessible by the CPU?
questions.explanation: The CPU can only fetch instructions and data from main memory (and registers/cache). Everything else is secondary/tertiary storage.

questions.text: The mechanism that allows high-speed I/O devices to transfer blocks of data directly to/from main memory without CPU intervention is:
questions.explanation: DMA allows the device controller to move data in blocks, generating only one interrupt per block rather than one per byte, saving CPU cycles.

questions.text: What is the primary advantage of Symmetric Multiprocessing (SMP) over Asymmetric Multiprocessing?

choices.label: Only one processor runs the kernel, making it simpler.
choices.text: Only one processor runs the kernel, making it simpler.

choices.label: Each processor can perform all tasks, providing better load balancing and reliability.
choices.text: Each processor can perform all tasks, providing better load balancing and reliability.

choices.label: It is much cheaper to implement.
choices.text: It is much cheaper to implement.

choices.label: It does not require a common bus.
choices.text: It does not require a common bus.

questions.explanation: In SMP, all processors are peers and can execute any task, unlike Asymmetric where one 'master' assigns specific tasks to 'slaves'.

questions.text: Clustered systems share storage via a:
questions.explanation: Clusters typically consist of multiple separate systems working together, sharing storage through a SAN for high availability.

questions.text: Multiprogramming increases CPU utilization by:

choices.label: Increasing the clock speed of the processor.
choices.text: Increasing the clock speed of the processor.

choices.label: Ensuring the CPU always has a job to execute by keeping a subset of jobs in memory.
choices.text: Ensuring the CPU always has a job to execute by keeping a subset of jobs in memory.

choices.label: Executing only one job until it completes.
choices.text: Executing only one job until it completes.

choices.label: Eliminating the need for I/O devices.
choices.text: Eliminating the need for I/O devices.

questions.explanation: Multiprogramming organizes jobs so that when one job must wait for I/O, the OS switches to another job, keeping the CPU busy.

questions.text: In dual-mode operation, the 'mode bit' is used to distinguish between:
questions.explanation: The hardware mode bit (0 for kernel, 1 for user) allows the system to protect itself by restricting privileged instructions to kernel mode.

questions.text: Which of the following instructions would be considered 'privileged'? (Select all that apply)

choices.label: Set the value of the timer
choices.text: Set the value of the timer

questions.explanation: Instructions that could harm the system or other users (timer settings, I/O control, mode bit changes, memory clearing) are privileged. Arithmetic is not.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'timer' in operating system operations?

choices.label: To display the current time to the user.
choices.text: To display the current time to the user.

choices.label: To prevent a user program from running forever or hogging resources.
choices.text: To prevent a user program from running forever or hogging resources.

choices.label: To synchronize data between the cache and memory.
choices.text: To synchronize data between the cache and memory.

choices.label: To increase the speed of disk scheduling.
choices.text: To increase the speed of disk scheduling.

questions.explanation: A timer is used to trigger an interrupt after a specific period, ensuring the OS regains control from user processes.

questions.text: A program is a ____ entity, while a process is an ____ entity.
questions.explanation: A program is just a file on disk (passive), whereas a process is a program in execution (active).

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a responsibility of the OS in memory management?

choices.label: Keeping track of which parts of memory are currently used
choices.text: Keeping track of which parts of memory are currently used

choices.label: Compiling source code into machine code
choices.text: Compiling source code into machine code

choices.label: Deciding which processes to move into memory
choices.text: Deciding which processes to move into memory

questions.explanation: Compiling is done by a compiler (application/system program), not the OS memory management module.

questions.text: Which kernel data structure provides O(lg n) search performance?
questions.explanation: Search in a standard list or unbalanced tree is O(n), but a balanced BST ensures O(log n) efficiency.

questions.text: In which computing environment do nodes act as both clients and servers without a central server?
questions.explanation: P2P systems like Napster or Gnutella treat all nodes as peers that can request or provide services.

questions.text: Cloud computing that provides a software stack ready for application use (like a database server) is referred to as:
questions.explanation: PaaS provides the platform (OS + middleware like DBs) for developers to run applications.

questions.text: What is the key requirement for an OS to be considered 'Real-Time'?

choices.label: It must have a high-speed GPU.
choices.text: It must have a high-speed GPU.

choices.label: It must have well-defined, fixed time constraints for processing.
choices.text: It must have well-defined, fixed time constraints for processing.

choices.label: It must run on a mobile device.
choices.text: It must run on a mobile device.

questions.explanation: Real-time systems are defined by their ability to complete tasks within strict timing deadlines.

questions.text: The 'copyleft' GNU Public License (GPL) is associated with which organization?
questions.explanation: The FSF started the open-source/free software movement and manages the GPL.

questions.text: What does a Virtual Machine Manager (VMM) provide?

choices.label: Virtualization services to run guest OSes
choices.text: Virtualization services to run guest OSes

questions.explanation: The VMM (also called a hypervisor) manages and abstracts hardware to allow multiple guest operating systems to run on a single host.
